observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the LSB
agreed to (a) continue with the required preparation and submission of the SEF
Budget and the posting of its utilization reports in at least three conspicuous public
places and regular posting in the FDPP, for transparency and accountability; and (b)
in coordination with the Municipal Accountant as the preparer of the report and the
LSB Co-chairman and Members as monitoring individuals, ensure the regular
submission of quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization, to the DepEd CO,
through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and
Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG pursuant to Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-
DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

2.10 We recommended and Management agreed that (i) the Municipal Accountant
make a thorough review of the details of the transactions affecting the Cash and Cash
Equivalents account and make the appropriate adjustments, if warranted in order to
present fairly all the affected accounts in the FS, (ii) the Accounting and Treasury
Offices maintain proper accounting of the Cash accounts and monitoring of the book
and bank reconciling items, and (iii) the Accounting Office consistently prepare and
submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements.

3.4 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Treasurer
(i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to Municipal Accountant at
every beginning of the year for recording and adjustment purposes; and (ii) record
and report collections of special education tax immediately in the cash books of SEF
and deposit collections directly to the SEF bank account; and (b) Municipal
Accountant (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified
by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) record the advance payments
when collected using the correct accounts and amounts; (iii) monitor the earned
revenue from advance payments in the previous year and make necessary
adjustments to recognize the realized/earned revenue during the current year; (iv)
record collections under SEF independently from GF to avoid delay in the
recognition of the special education tax and to stop the incurrence of discrepancy
between the balances of the related accounts; (v) be extra careful in recording
collections from RPT/SET especially the account to be used and the amount to be
recognized; (vi) debit the Deferred RPT/SET and credit the RPT/SET Receivables
accounts for the total amount of collections made by the PGT in behalf of the
Municipality; (vii) conduct a thorough analysis of the errors incurred and make the
necessary adjustments to correct all the aforementioned misstatements; and (viii)
align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the
MNGAS for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT/SET transactions.

4.29 We recommended and Management agreed that the (i) Inventory Committee
conduct a complete semi-annual physical count of inventories and submit the
corresponding RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance
with Section 124 of the MNGAs for LGUs and exhaust all possible remedies to identify
and locate the composition of the Municipality’s non-moving inventories; (ii)
Municipal Accountant ensure proper accounting treatment for the purchase of
supplies and materials by recognizing inventory items as expenses only upon
consumption or distribution, and reconcile the year-end balances of inventories based
on the submitted RPCI; and (iii) Accounting Office and General Services Office (a)
maintain the SCs, SPCs, SLPCs and SPLC for all LGU inventories and observe the
periodic reconciliation of the said records to monitor agency assets properly; (b)
maintain an SSMI and RSPI to serve as a basis for preparing Journal Entries to
record the supplies and materials and semi-expendable properties issued; (c)
maintain a RegSPI to ensure proper monitoring of semi-expendable properties and
(d) maintain a WMR to establish transparency regarding the legitimacy of
replacements and disposal of waste materials.

5.29 We recommended and the Inventory Committee, Accounting Office and the
Property Officer, as the case may be, agreed to: (i) conduct a complete annual
physical count of PPEs and come up with the RPCPPE in accordance with Section
124 of MNGAS for LGU; (ii) maintain the PCs and PPELCs for all LGUs properties;
(iii) observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency’s assets
properly; (iv) maintain an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property to properly document and track the unserviceable assets of the Municipality;
(v) ) conform with the implementing guidelines of Section 259 of GAM Vol. 1 for
LGUs by assigning the responsibility for generating and safekeeping PAR to the
Property Section; (vi) and establish and maintain an organized filing system to
facilitate efficient tracking, retrieval, and verification of custodianship; and (vii)
implement the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account balances in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address discrepancies between recorded balances and
actual physical assets.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal
Accountant recognize leave benefits quarterly as they are earned, rather than
recording the entire liability in a single year; (b) Municipal Budget Officer establish an
Employee Benefits Reserve Fund to fund for all the leave benefits owed to
employees; and (c) Municipal Human Resource Management Office to coordinate
with the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Budget Office to ensure
adherence to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive
Order (EO) No. 292 on the yearly accounting and funding of leave benefits.

7.6 We reiterated our recommendation and the Accounting Office agreed to
strictly conform to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01,
2015 in the recording of transactions.

8.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to book the 20%
LGU share from Fire Code Revenues in the GF and the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
subject its utilization under the process of regular appropriation through the
approval of local Sanggunian.

9.16 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Local Chief Executive
(LCE) continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by
appropriating 20% of its annual NTA pursuant to the provisions of the DILG-DBM
JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and JMC No. 01 dated November 04, 2020;
(b) BAC together with the Technical Working Group (TWG), (i) to prepare the BAC
resolution declaring LCRB and recommending approval only after the post-
qualification evaluation reports; (ii) submit the lacking supporting documents, as
discussed, and ensure the completeness of documentation before the processing of
payment of claim/s by the contractor/s; and (c) Municipal Accountant closely review
the recorded transactions in accordance with the revised chart of accounts for LGUs
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

10.12 We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the (a)
Municipal Accountant (i) make a thorough review of the details of the transactions
affecting the LDRRMF STF as presented in the subsidiary ledger for the Trust
Liability – DRRMF account; (ii) practice consistency in strictly conforming to the
proper guidelines in determining the amount of unexpended/unobligated balance of
the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF in order to avoid misstatement of
the LDRRMF STF; (iii) make the appropriate adjustments, if warranted in order to
present fairly the affected accounts in the FS; and (iv) disclose in the Notes to the FS
complete and necessary data for disaster risk reduction and management
transactions; and (b) MDRRMC carefully plan, implement and prepare the
LDRRMFIP so that it contains all required information in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2012-002 and list of projects to be charged against the unexpended fund
of prior years.

11.12 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (i) intensify and strategize tax
collection efforts from business tax payers to surpass the target business tax collection
for the year.

11.13 We also reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Treasurer (i)
continue to comply and enforce the 2019 Revised Revenue Code so that correct
business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality; (ii) require the submission
of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipts, not just mere certifications
from company officers, and copy of income tax return, to ensure that the basis
declared by the business establishments are reliable and accurate; and (iii) examine
the books of businesses to ascertain the correctness of assessment and collection of
business tax.

12.17 We recommended and the GFPS agreed to (i) strive for the full
implementation of the identified and approved PPAs by utilizing judiciously the fund
set aside for said purposes; (ii) observe the budget limits of expenditures per planned
activity; (iii) inform the concerned DILG Office in writing regarding the changes in
the DILG-endorsed GPB to ensure that the actual results conformed with established
performance indicator and target; and (iv) assure that attribution is made using the
HGDG tool and regard it in the “attributed programs” section of the enhanced GPB
and GAD AR template.

13.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant (i) institute a layer of review in
recording adjustments to avoid committing further errors; (ii) make the necessary
adjustments to correct all the aforementioned misstatements; and (iii) strictly conform to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, in the recording
and reporting of transactions.

14.7 We recommended and a) Management agreed to design a more effective
strategy that will allow a timelier recording of transactions to facilitate the
reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and remitted; and b) Municipal
Accountant (i) provide a separate schedule detailing the discrepancies between the
monthly withheld and remitted amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding
of the amounts posted in the books of accounts; and (ii) make the appropriate
adjustments in order to present fairly the Due to BIR account.

15.6 We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the (a)
HRMO Office and Accounting Office take the necessary measures to lessen or
eliminate the over and/or under remittances of the balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-
IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts; and the (b) Municipal Accountant provide a separate
schedule detailing the discrepancies between the monthly withheld and remitted
amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding of the amounts posted in the
books of accounts.

We recommended and the:
(i) Accounting and Treasury Offices agreed to exercise prudence in recording and monitoring transactions affecting the cash accounts and utilize the proper source documents to ensure the reliability of recorded amounts; and
(ii) Accounting Office continuously prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements and warrant appropriate adjustments immediately.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to: (i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to Municipal Accountant every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) make the necessary adjustments to account the proper treatment on tax collections; and (iii) align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the NGAS Manual for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT transactions.

We recommended and the (a) Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the: (i) concerned offices to prepare a schedule of the account certified by the Accountant and approved by the Local Chief Executive, provide Certification by Legal Officer that there are no pending cases relative to the account, provide Certification by the responsible officials to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim, and consider reverting the dormant balances; and (ii) Municipal Accountant to strictly conform to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 in the recording of transactions; and (b) Management return the unused balance of fund transfers to the source agencies in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the: (a) BAC and Accounting Office to submit the lacking supporting documents, as discussed, and ensure the completeness of documentation before the processing of payment of claim/s by the contractor/s; and (b) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to comply with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 specifically in ensuring that that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation-ready.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly conform to the proper guidelines in determining the amount of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF in order to avoid misstatement of the LDRRMF STF; (ii) make a thorough review of the details of the transactions affecting the LDRRMF STF and make the appropriate adjustments, if warranted in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the FS; and (iii) reclassify the balance of the LDRRMF STF to the Trust Liability – DRRMF account in conformity to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2015-009; (b) MDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMFIP in the format as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (c) Municipal Accountant to present the minimum required disclosure in the NFS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002. We also recommended and the (a) LCE agreed to refrain from granting CA for expenses not amongst the allowed purposes of CA, instead, require the disbursing officer to pay expenses through check under the name of the supplier; (b) BAC ensure compliance to the general and specific guidelines for alternative methods of procurement; (c) Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before recording the liquidation of CA; and (d) concerned personnel submit the lacking documents and refund the amount of ₱5,226.00.

We commended Management for the efficient collection performance and recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (i) continue to comply and enforce the 2019 Revised Revenue Code so that correct business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality and (ii) require the submission of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the business establishments are reliable and accurate; and (iii) examine the books of businesses to ascertain the correctness of assessment and collection of business tax.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to: (i) facilitate the proper planning of programs that can maximize their funds to facilitate intervention in addressing gender issues in the Municipality; (ii) equip themselves with the HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Agency's actual disbursements in implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD; and (iii) warrant the preparation and submission of the three-year GAD Agenda.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to (i) institute a layer of review in recording adjustments to avoid committing further errors; (ii) make the necessary adjustments to correct all the aforementioned misstatements; and (iii) strictly conform to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, in the recording and reporting of transactions.

We recommended and: (a) Management agreed to design a more effective strategy that will allow a timelier recording of transactions to facilitate the reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and remitted; and (b) Municipal Accountant: (i) Provide a separate schedule detailing the discrepancies between the monthly withheld and remitted amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding of the amounts posted in the books of accounts; and (ii) Make the appropriate adjustments in order to present fairly the Due to BIR account.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the: (a) HRMO Office and Accounting Office take the necessary measures to lessen or eliminate the over and/or under remittances of the balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts; and the (b) Municipal Accountant provide a separate schedule detailing the discrepancies between the monthly withheld and remitted amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding of the amounts posted in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the: 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) furnish the updated RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to Municipal Accountant every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and 

(ii) intensify and strategize tax collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places, and tapping the assistance of the barangay officials for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; and 

(ii) align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the MNGAS for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to: 

i) order the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory taking in the ensuing year and prepare and submit RPCPPE in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs; 

ii) direct the Accounting Office to exert more effort to reconcile PPELCs with the GLs and secure the necessary documents and reports so that adequate depreciation of all depreciable PPE items can be provided; and 

iii) instruct the GSO and Accounting Office to prepare a reconciliation of their records and to perform the cleansing count eventually.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the: 

(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure proper classification of Loans Payable in the SFPos by presenting the portion due to be settled within 12 months after the reporting date as Current Liabilities and the remaining portion as Non-Current Liabilities; 

(ii) disclose adequately all the necessary information in the NFS; and 

(iii) make the necessary adjustments to account the proper recording of collections from the barangays; and 

(b) Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the opening of a TF account devoted for the payments of loans received from the barangays.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

(i) return the unused balance of fund transfers to the SAs in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and 

(ii) remit the amount due to the BTr.

Further, we recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Accounting Office, Budget Officer, and other concerned Offices to: 

(i) exhaust all means to locate documents pertaining to the dormant fund transfers, determine whether the purposes can still be achieved, and 

(ii) consider the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: 

i) present the minimum required disclosure in the NFS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002; and 

ii) check and ensure the completeness and reliability of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended that: 

i) LSB formulate the required three-year program so that utilization of the SEF would be maximized through coordination with the Municipal and Provincial investment and development plans; 

ii) Municipal Accountant and MBO determine the unutilized appropriations and cause the inclusion of the same in preparing the SEF annual budget; and 

iii) the Municipality coordinate with the DepEd regarding the monitoring of accountabilities of DepEd employees who are being reassigned and/or terminating their employment with the said agency, for the proper return or turnover of properties issued to them.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GFPS and Focal Person to: 

(i) strive for the full implementation of the identified and approved PPAs by utilizing judiciously the fund set aside for said purposes; and 

(ii) prioritize the adoption of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law.

1.8 We recommended and the (i) Accounting and Treasury
Offices agreed to
exercise prudence in recording and monitoring transactions affecting the cash
accounts and utilize the proper source documents to ensure the reliability of recorded
amounts; and (ii) Accounting Office continuously prepare and submit the monthly
bank reconciliation statements and warrant appropriate adjustments immediately.

2.17 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the
(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to
Municipal Accountant every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and (b)
Municipal Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list
duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) make the
necessary adjustments to account the proper treatment on tax collections; and (iii)
align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the
NGAS Manual for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT transactions.

3.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct
the Municipal Accountant to (a) comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-
GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, use and
reporting of the CFs by (i) submitting the CA vouchers within the prescribed period;
and (ii) liquidating the CA for CF at year-end; and (b) use the proper accounts in the
recognition and liquidation of CA and make the necessary adjustments to
account the proper recording of CAs for CF.

4.12 We recommended and the Inventory Committee, Accounting Office and the
Property Officer, as the case may be, agreed to: (i) conduct a complete annual
physical count of PPEs and come up with the RPCPPE in accordance with Section
124 of MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) maintain the Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards
for all LGUs properties and observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to
monitor agency’s assets properly.

5.11 We recommended and the (a) the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
(i) concerned offices to prepare a schedule of the account certified by the Accountant
and approved by the Local Chief Executive, provide Certification by Legal Officer
that there are no pending cases relative to the account, provide Certification by the
responsible officials to the effect that there are no records/documents available to
validate claim, and consider reverting the dormant balances; and (ii) Municipal
Accountant to strictly conform to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 01, 2015 in the recording of transactions; and (b) Management return the
unused balance of fund transfers to the source agencies in compliance with Section
6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

7.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant to (i) strictly conform to the proper guidelines in determining the amount
of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the
LDRRMF in order to avoid misstatement of the LDRRMF STF; (ii) make a thorough
review of the details of the transactions affecting the LDRRMF STF and make the
appropriate adjustments, if warranted in order to present fairly the affected accounts
in the FS; and (iii) reclassify the balance of the LDRRMF STF to the Trust Liability
– DRRMF account in conformity to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and
COA Circular No. 2015-009; (b) MDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMFIP in the
format as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (c) Municipal Accountant
to present the minimum required disclosure in the NFS as specified in COA Circular
No. 2014-002.

7.13 We also recommended and the (a) LCE agreed to refrain from granting CA
for expenses not amongst the allowed purposes of CA, instead, require the disbursing
officer to pay expenses through check under the name of the supplier; (b) BAC ensure
compliance to the general and specific guidelines for alternative methods of
procurement; (c) Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting
documents before recording the liquidation of CA; and (d) concerned personnel
submit the lacking documents and refund the amount of ₱5,226.00.

8.12 We commended Management for the efficient collection performance and
recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to (i) continue to comply and enforce the 2019 Revised Revenue Code so
that correct business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality and (ii) require
the submission of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of
income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the business establishments are
reliable and accurate; and (iii) examine the books of businesses to ascertain the
correctness of assessment and collection of business tax.

9.12 We recommended and the GFPS agreed to (i) facilitate the proper planning of
programs that can maximize their funds to facilitate intervention in addressing
gender issues in the Municipality; (ii) equip themselves with the HGDG tool and
PIMME to determine how much of the Agency's actual disbursements in
implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD; and (iii) warrant the
preparation and submission of the three-year GAD Agenda.

10.14 We commended the Municipality in their continuous efforts in addressing
solid waste management and we recommended and the Management agreed to direct
the MSWMB to (i) prioritize the update of the SWMP to ensure its sustainability,
viability and effectiveness, and and (ii) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budgets
of the Municipality to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully achieved.

11.7 However, we further recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a)
Accounting Office and the Human Resource and Management Office to Office to take
the necessary measures to lessen or eliminate the over and under remittances of the
balances of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, and (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) provide a separate schedule detailing the discrepancies between the
monthly withheld and remitted amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding
of the amounts posted in the books of accounts and; (b) make the appropriate
adjustments, if warranted in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant agreed to regularly reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records so that errors committed can be identified and appropriate adjustments in the records can be effected immediately.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents for the receivables noted. Henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant accounts for more than 10 years, in accordance with the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005. 

We also recommended and Management agreed to further investigate and gather supporting documents such as the settlement of the audit disallowances/charges to record the derecognition of the balance of Receivables - Disallowances/Charges account.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of DF so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented.

We also reiterated that the LCE instruct the: 

(a) BAC and its Secretariat to fully comply with the provision of the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184 on the (i) posting of Contracts and NTPs; (ii) inviting observers during bid evaluations and post-qualification activities; and (iii) sending of notifications of bidding results to losing bidders; 

(b) BAC and TWG to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion; and 

(c) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

Lastly, we recommended that BAC Secretariat and concerned officials submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five days from the execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: 

(i) maintain special accounts in the GF for the LGU’s LEEs and 

(ii) prepare the complete set of FS thereof as prescribed in the Manual so that an accurate basis in managing the operations related thereto will be made.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue the good practice of adhering to the applicable rules and regulations in the withholding and remittance of taxes and contributions/other payables due to appropriate agencies. 

In addition, we recommended and Management agreed to: 

(i) reconcile records with concerned agencies if discrepancies are discovered in the monthly statement of account/billing to avoid the need for refunds; and 

(ii) adopt a solution that will facilitate the timely recording and reporting of transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive and inclusive LDRRMFIP particularly disclosing the list of initiatives charged against the unexpended calamity fund transferred to special trust account; and

4. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to  direct  the
   (a) Bids and Awards  Committee  (BAC),  BAC  Secretariat  and  Accounting
   Office to (i) submit the lacking documents as enumerated,  in  compliance
   with the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001;  and
   (ii) address the other issues in the supporting documents  noted  by  the
   Audit Team.

5. We also recommended and  Management  agreed  to  (i)  avoid  engaging  in
   transactions  that  may  fall  under  the  definition  of  splitting   of
   contracts; ii) stop the practice of paying  claims  thru  reimbursements;
   and (iii) adhere to the general principles of imprest  fund  system  that
   all payments, particularly on procured goods, be paid  through  check  in
   the name of the supplier.

14. We recommended and the  LCE  agreed  to  instruct  the  (a)  Municipal
      Treasurer to update the RPTARs/Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently,
      prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning  of  the
      year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and  collectible
      during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in  setting-up
      the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year;  and (b)  Municipal
      Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced  from  the
      list duly certified by the Treasury Office at  the  beginning  of  the
      year; and (ii) make the necessary adjustments to properly account  the
      remittance of Barangay Share.

the concerned Municipal officials and personnel - submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods; and
the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the fundamentals of recording purchases of materials and equipment.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies to foster compliance to existing rules and promote transparency and accountability in the administration of donations intended to augment the needs of the constituents during the lockdown/quarantine period.

the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods.
to continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented;
that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office, and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents.

We recommended and Management agreed that all concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality continue to withhold and remit the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHIC.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
the concerned Municipal officials and personnel (i) submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and (ii) observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods; and
the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the fundamentals of recording purchases of materials and equipment.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies to foster compliance to existing rules and promote transparency and accountability in the administration of donations intended to augment the needs of the constituents during the lockdown/quarantine period.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive and inclusive LDRRMFIP particularly disclosing the list of initiatives charged against the unexpended calamity fund transferred to special trust account; and
the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods.

We recommended and Management agreed (i) to continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; and (ii) that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office, and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents.
We also reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities.

We recommended and Management agreed that all concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality continue to withhold and remit the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHIC.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously exert extra efforts to retrieve the relevant documents and settle the identified issues on the reconciliation of the cash accounts records.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.managementAction:
Inventory Team has been created and conduct of physical count and preparation of RPCPPE is ongoing.


The concerned offices are exerting utmost effort to recover records to determine the accuracy of the reported carrying value of municipal properties.
Ledger Card has been prepared for each PPE to properly determine the accuracy of depreciation.

observations.recommendation:

Management agreed to continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented;

Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents; and

direct the MDRRMO to 

procure the tools and equipment prescribed in the Disaster Preparedness Manual which are not yet available in the agency; 

always prepare a comprehensive and inclusive LDRRMFIP particularly disclosing the list of initiatives charged against the unexpended calamity fund transferred to special trust account; and
 
require the concerned offices to submit the lacking documents.
