direct all concerned personnel to expedite the review and submission of documents pertaining to their transactions so as not to cause delays; 

direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the interim financial statements and submit them quarterly; and 

strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to 

work on the proper identification of its members and consider adopting the functions, roles and responsibilities of GFPS, LCE, Executive Committee, TWG and Secretariat in full compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC 2016-01 and 

ensure that the GAD Accomplishment Report is properly supported with all the required reports.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality continue to withhold and remit the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHIC.

We reiterated our recommendation and Municipal Accountant agreed to exert extra efforts to retrieve the relevant documents and settle the identified issues on the reconciliation of the cash accounts records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

observations.reason: The Municipality did not procure common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) from DBM-Procurement Service during the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously exert extra efforts to retrieve the relevant documents and settle the identified issues on the reconciliation of the cash accounts records.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) direct the Inventory Team to conduct physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team; (b) require the Accounting and Treasury Offices to prepare and update PPE ledger cards for each PPE sub-account so that general ledger balances of PPE are supported by independent and reliable records; (c) instruct the Accounting Office to exert best effort to secure the necessary documents and reports so that appropriate depreciation can be provided; and (d) direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to recover the records that would determine the carrying value of the demolished structure and thereafter derecognize them in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and the -
Management agreed to continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented;
Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents; and
Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
direct the MDRRMO to (i) procure the tools and equipment prescribed in the Disaster Preparedness Manual which are not yet available in the agency; (ii) always prepare a comprehensive and inclusive LDRRMFIP particularly disclosing the list of initiatives charged against the unexpended calamity fund transferred to special trust account; and
require the concerned offices to submit the lacking documents.

We recommended and Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) direct all concerned personnel to expedite the review and submission of documents pertaining to their transactions so as not to cause delays; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the interim financial statements and submit them quarterly; and (c) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to (i) work on the proper identification of its members and consider adopting the functions, roles and responsibilities of GFPS, LCE, Executive Committee, TWG and Secretariat in full compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC 2016-01 and (ii) ensure that the GAD Accomplishment Report is properly supported with all the required reports.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality continue to withhold and remit the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHIC.

We recommended and Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the - 

Designated Property Officer to: 

cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property together with the assessment, evaluation and appraisal reports; and 

undertake immediate disposal of the subject properties to prevent further deterioration and decline in value; and

Municipal Accountant to derecognize the motor vehicle in the books of accounts of the Municipality by virtue of a valid donation.

We also reiterated the audit recommendation and Local Chief Executive agreed to: 

create an inventory team to conduct physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and 

require the Accounting and Treasury Office to complete their property ledger cards/records and conduct the reconciliation together with the RPCPPE.

We recommended that management continue to: 

abide with the terms and conditions of the loan agreement, 

pay religiously the loan amortizations, and 

effect the adjustment to reconcile the loan balances with the Land Bank of the Philippines’ records.

We recommended and management agreed to: 

adopt better planning strategies in order to comprehensively consider all risk factors in programming, prioritizing and implementing developmental projects funded by donors/sponsoring agencies; and
 
ensure that compliance with the documentation, reporting standards and requirements provided in the DILG Circulars are satisfactorily observed within the prescribed timelines.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct –
 
the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office to observe the instructions on contract submission prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01 and 

BAC to follow the provision on observers’ invitation as provided in RA No. 9184.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal LSB to stop the practice of utilizing the SEF for expenses unrelated to or expressly provided in Sections 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to – 

undergo GAD-oriented seminars and trainings; and 

submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period in order to facilitate timely audit of the Municipality’s GAD activities and accomplishments.

We reiterated our audit recommendation and management agreed to:

strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and 

observe the prescribed period for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
We recommended and management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Human Resource and Management Officer to exert their conscionable effort in gathering all pertinent documents in order to analyze the subject accounts and settle the unreconciled balances of ₱47,974.81 of Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth accounts and negative balance of ₱33,863.40 of Due to Pag-IBIG account.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the:

Municipal Accountant (i) maintain PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) exert efforts to secure the necessary documents and reports so that appropriate depreciation can be provided.

Municipality designate a Property Officer for the maintenance of property cards for all PPE; and

Municipality create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.
We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to document and settle reconciling figures and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Accountant (i) strengthen his review controls over disbursements and ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents before certifying the DVs; and (ii) deliver immediately the accounts and reports that are due for submission as of date. We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to defer the approval or processing of the check if the DV does not bear signatures of the key officials.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate timely audit of the Municipality’s GAD activities and accomplishments.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts for each Local Economic Enterprise of the Municipality in the General Fund.
observations.reason: Non-compliance of the audit recommendation is still observable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office regularly reconcile its records with the GSIS, settle the difference, and immediately make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation and Municipal Accountant agreed to exert extra efforts to retrieve the relevant documents and settle the identified issues on the reconciliation of the cash accounts records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the -
Designated Property Officer to (i) cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property together with the assessment, evaluation and appraisal reports; and (ii) undertake immediate disposal of the subject properties to prevent further deterioration and decline in value; and
Municipal Accountant to derecognize the motor vehicle in the books of accounts of the Municipality by virtue of a valid donation.
We also reiterated the audit recommendation and Local Chief Executive agreed to:
create an inventory team to conduct physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and
require the Accounting and Treasury Office to complete their property ledger cards/records and conduct the reconciliation together with the RPCPPE.

We recommended that management continue to abide with the terms and conditions of the loan agreement, pay religiously the loan amortizations, and effect the adjustment to reconcile the loan balances with the Land Bank of the Philippines’ records.

We recommended and management agreed to adopt better planning strategies in order to comprehensively consider all risk factors in programming, prioritizing and implementing developmental projects funded by donors/sponsoring agencies and ensure that compliance with the documentation, reporting standards and requirements provided in the DILG Circulars are satisfactorily observed within the prescribed timelines.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct (i) the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office to observe the instructions on contract submission prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01 and (ii) BAC to follow the provision on observers’ invitation as provided in RA No. 9184.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal LSB to stop the practice of utilizing the SEF for expenses unrelated to or expressly provided in Sections 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that:
Management consider to fill up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions and employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs; and
Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to ensure compliance with respect to the minimum standards on disaster preparedness.
We also recommended that:
Management observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents, before, during or after disaster/calamities;
Accountant to observe proper controls in recording the purchases of relief goods and require the responsible officers to submit appropriate documents supporting the distribution of the goods;
Local Chief Executive spearhead to stop the practice of payment of various expenses through reimbursement and ensure that all payments must be made by check directly to the suppliers; and
Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents above-mentioned.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to (a) undergo GAD-oriented seminars and trainings; and (b) submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period in order to facilitate timely audit of the Municipality’s GAD activities and accomplishments.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Bids and Awards Committee to procure the common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) from Procurement Service of the DBM and ensure that procurement of CSEs other than the PS depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in the PS depot should be supported by a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS).

We reiterated our audit recommendation and management agreed to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the prescribed period for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
We recommended and management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Human Resource and Management Officer to exert their conscionable effort in gathering all pertinent documents in order to analyze the subject accounts and settle the unreconciled balances of ₱47,974.81 of Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth accounts and negative balance of ₱33,863.40 of Due to Pag-IBIG account.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider the negotiation with government bank to enroll in the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system to lessen the risks of loss, strengthen controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency in treasury operations.

We recommended and Management agreed that (a) the Municipality and the DepEd Schools Division Office, consider establishing a mutually agreed policy or agreement on managing SEF property; (b) the LCE instruct the Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain complete property records and reports; and (c) the Local Chief Executive consider transferring, by way of donation, to the DepEd, the SEF-funded properties which are movable and intended for use directly in the classroom so that the person in actual physical possession/control of the SEF property is made responsible for its proper use and safekeeping.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that Property Officer is still not maintaining complete property records and reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to (a) stop the practice of granting to elective official cash advances other than cash advances for travel expenses and instead grant any necessary cash advance to a permanent appointed official who can act as a proper accountable officer duly authorized by the Local Chief Executive; (b) ensure that Accountable Officers whose cash advances exceeded their maximum cash accountability increase their amount of Fidelity Bond and refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers beyond their maximum cash accountability; (c) direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 4 (6) of the P.D. No. 1445 and the rules prescribed in the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 by checking the completeness of supporting documents before any payment is effected; (d) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders and Inspection and Acceptance Reports be accomplished properly; and (e) refrain from directly paying the excess of expenses from the amount of cash advances without proper accounting of the liquidation of the previous cash advance.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) strictly conform with the proper guidelines in determining the amount of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF in order to avoid understatement or overstatement of the Special Trust Fund and to transfer an additional unexpended/unobligated balance of ₱276,415.31 DRRM Fund to the Special Trust Fund; (b) ensure the completeness of their Monthly Report of Utilization to guarantee its reliability and effectiveness as the main tool of the Municipality for its proper monitoring of the LDRRM Fund; and (c)  implement the programs and projects which were originally funded by the LDRRM Continuing Fund or realign them to new programs or projects if deemed necessary. 

We also recommended and Management agreed to consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions; employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs and comply with the minimum standards and existing rules and regulations on disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
observations.reason: Non-compliance with some audit recommendations were reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that before the implementation of the project, they will ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program is duly approved by DOLE wherein its cost is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record as Inventory account all purchases of drugs and medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies; (ii) properly evaluate the correctness of accounting entries prior to recording in the books of the Municipality; and (b) BAC or concerned office to furnish copies of POs to the Audit Team within five days after issuance and copies of Inspection and Acceptance or its equivalent within 24 hours after their receipt of goods.
observations.reason: The BAC did not furnish copies of POs and Inspection and Acceptance to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office exert effort in ensuring the completeness and accuracy of prepared remittances; to remit the same within the prescribed period; to regularly reconcile its records with PHIC and Pag-IBIG; and immediately make the necessary adjustments, if necessary.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accounting Office to ensure the completeness of supporting documents on Disbursement Vouchers before payment to avoid delays in the proper preparation of monthly tax remittance schedules and to remit all withheld taxes intact within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the OIC, Municipal Accountant exert more efforts to gather documents pertinent to the so-called reconciling figures to be able to identify them and make the necessary adjustment.  If no documents are found, adopt the bank balance as the correct balance if it is more than the book balance, otherwise, the persons in-charge of preparing the bank reconciliation statements and recording cash transactions in the general ledger at the time the undetected reconciling items occurred will be held liable therefor.

We reiterated our recommendation that the:
Municipal Accountant maintain PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;
Designated Property Officer maintain property cards for all PPE; and
c. Inventory Committee exert extra effort to conduct physical count of property, plant and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of drugs and medicines in the books using the Inventory account; (b) Concerned Offices to furnish copies of POs to the Audit Team within five days after issuance of copies of Inspection and Acceptance or its equivalent within 24 hours after their receipt of goods; and (c) Bids and Awards Committee to consolidate all procurement requests (PPMPs) and prepare the APP.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) prioritize the implementation of projects, activities and programs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

1.4	We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) maintain PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) exert efforts to secure the necessary documents and reports so that appropriate depreciation can be provided.
Municipality designate a Property Officer for the maintenance of property cards for all PPE; and
Municipality create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.

2.6	We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to document and settle reconciling figures and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted.

3.7	We recommended and Management agreed to consider the negotiation with government bank to enroll in the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system to lessen the risks of loss; strengthen controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency in treasury operations.

5.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the Accountant (i) strengthen his review controls over disbursements and ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents before certifying the DVs; and (ii) deliver immediately the accounts and reports that are due for submission as of date. We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to defer the approval or processing of the check if the DV does not bear signatures of the key officials.

6.1	We recommended and Management agreed to (a) stop the practice of granting to elective official cash advances other than cash advances for travel expenses and instead grant any necessary cash advance to a permanent appointed official who can act as a proper accountable officer duly authorized by the Local Chief Executive; (b) ensure that Accountable Officers whose cash advances exceeded their maximum cash accountability increase their amount of Fidelity Bond and refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers beyond their maximum cash accountability; (c) direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 4 (6) of the P.D. No. 1445 and the rules prescribed in the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 by checking the completeness of supporting documents before any payment is effected; (d) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders and Inspection and Acceptance Reports be accomplished properly; and (e) refrain from directly paying the excess of expenses from the amount of cash advances without proper accounting of the liquidation of the previous cash advance.

7.6	We recommended and Management agreed to (a) strictly conform with the proper guidelines in determining the amount of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF in order to avoid understatement or overstatement of the Special Trust Fund and to transfer an additional unexpended/unobligated balance of ₱276,415.31 DRRM Fund to the Special Trust Fund; (b) ensure the completeness of their Monthly Report of Utilization to guarantee its reliability and effectiveness as the main tool of the Municipality for its proper monitoring of the LDRRM Fund; and (c)  implement the programs and projects which were originally funded by the LDRRM Continuing Fund or realign them to new programs or projects if deemed necessary.
7.7	We also recommended and Management agreed to consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions; employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs and comply with the minimum standards and existing rules and regulations on disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

8.11	We recommended and Management agreed that before the implementation of the project, they will ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program is duly approved by DOLE wherein its cost is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.

9.4	We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record as Inventory account all purchases of drugs and medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies; (ii) properly evaluate the correctness of accounting entries prior to recording in the books of the Municipality; and (b) BAC or concerned office to furnish copies of POs to the Audit Team within five days after issuance and copies of Inspection and Acceptance or its equivalent within 24 hours after their receipt of goods.

10.8	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate timely audit of the Municipality’s GAD activities and accomplishments.
11.6	We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts for each Local Economic Enterprise of the Municipality in the General Fund.

12.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office exert effort in ensuring the completeness and accuracy of prepared remittances; to remit the same within the prescribed period; to regularly reconcile its records with PHIC and Pag-IBIG; and immediately make the necessary adjustments, if necessary.

13.5	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Accounting Office to ensure the completeness of supporting documents on Disbursement Vouchers before payment to avoid delays in the proper preparation of monthly tax remittance schedules and to remit all withheld taxes intact within the prescribed period.

14.6	We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office regularly reconcile its records with the GSIS, settle the difference, and immediately make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to persons not connected or permanent appointed official of the Municipality to avoid risk of non-liquidation of cash advances and possible loss of government funds thru misuse or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor shall prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.  Avail of the legal remedies to collect from the delinquent real property taxpayers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Chairman and Members of the BAC to follow strictly the provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 or the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completed documented transactions/ disbursements shall be paid.

We recommended that the (a) BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices of the LGU of Moncada to (i) strictly monitor the implementation of projects as targeted and planned and take note of any gap for future inputs; (ii) adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR; (b) Local Chief Executive (i) direct the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completely documented transactions/ disbursements shall be paid; (ii) create a good internal communication protocols so that the concerned implementing office will become aware of the downloaded funds to avoid delays in the mobilization and implementation of projects; (c) Municipal Agriculturist hasten (i) the submission of the additional documents required by DA for the release of fund for the Livestock Program; (ii) the preparation of the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and farmers so that they can enjoy the benefits of the program at the earliest opportunity; (d) concerned Municipal Officials and Personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and examination.

We recommended that the required Monthly Report of Utilization be prepared and submitted on a regular basis.  We also recommended the transfer of unexpended/ unobligated balance of ₱3,475,664.43 DRRM Fund to the Special Trust Fund
observations.reason: Reports on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund were submitted but lacking in data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the members of the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the annual School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and activities as stipulated in Section 1 of RA No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April, 1998 and No. 01-A dated March, 2000; (b) stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of expenses not related to or included in the list of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF; and (c) strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate the Municipality’s own implementing guidelines, policies and procedures regarding AICS, in conformity with the provisions set forth by the DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014, as basis in the granting of financial or in-kind assistance to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in giving financial assistance (a) strictly adhere to the provisions set forth under DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014; and (b) use the assessment report of the MSWDO in determining the amount to be given to the beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated under Section 4.2 of DSWD MC No. 02, S. 2014 to establish the validity of the claims; (b) make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the AICS; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the proper account is used in the payment of AICS.

We recommended that members of the Sangguniang Bayan: (a) initiate the conduct of a thorough study of the roads existing within the Municipality and determine the availability of alternative routs; and (b) enact an appropriate ordinance providing an exception to the existing prohibition of three-wheeled public conveyances operating along the national highways within the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all municipal officials involved in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and policies to improve the proficiencies of the GFPS and TWG by creating GAD Database and GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: GAD Database was set up but GAD Plan, Budget and Accomplishment Report was not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to persons not connected or permanent appointed official of the Municipality to avoid risk of non-liquidation of cash advances and possible loss of government funds thru misuse or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor shall prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain reports/property records such as RPCPPE and Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation of accounting records with physical count will be made. Likewise, facilitate the conduct of physical count of all the properties of the Municipality with the Auditor as witness.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the contractor to post the necessary warranty security as prescribed under Section 62 of the IRR-A of R.A. No. 9184 before final payment is made to compensate any damages due to faulty or defective completion of infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Verification by the audit team revealed that no warranty security was attached to the disbursement vouchers for the payment of infrastructure contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local SB Members submit documents to support their actual performance of work to determine if their claim for RATA is in conformity with the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103.
observations.reason: Verification by the audit team revealed that evidence of actual performance such as attendance to regular sessions and/or copies of the minutes of meeting were not attached to the claims of SB members’ RATA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the remaining unexpended balance amounting to ₱4,342,151.66 be transferred from the General Fund bank account to the Trust Fund bank account in compliance with the above cited provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for proper monitoring of the utilization of the LDRRMF and to enable the LDRRMC to continue using the funds for five (5) years more from date of release.
We recommended that the LDRRMC comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the submission of the required report for the timely evaluation of the DRRMF.

We recommended that the LDRRMC prioritize the implementation of  projects, activities and programs on prevention, mitigation, and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant support disbursements of funds for services rendered by job order and service contract personnel with the necessary and appropriate supporting documents in accordance with rules and regulations to establish the validity and propriety of payments made to them.
observations.reason: Verification by the audit team revealed that accomplishment reports were not attached to the claims of Job Order personnel for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat submit the approved contracts and purchase orders within five days from execution, together with supporting documents, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order for the Audit Team to provide timely and systematic review and post-audit of procurement.
observations.reason: Verification by the audit team revealed that approved contracts and purchase orders together with their supporting documents were not submitted to the audit team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all municipal officials involved in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and policies to improve the proficiencies of the GFPS and TWG by creating GAD Database and GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the subject account, including the retrieval of the necessary transaction documents, to establish the accuracy of the year-end balance of ₱2,488,570.82 and to remit all withheld taxes intact within the prescribed period.  We likewise recommended that the forwarded balance of ₱1,156,619.44 which remain in the books be remitted, if the funds therefor are available.
observations.reason: As per Municipal Accountant, verification of taxes withheld & remittances from prior year’s transactions is in process before making payment of the remaining balance acquired from prior year’s transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the SEF will only be utilized for priority expenditures identified under the pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

1.5	We recommended that the OIC, Municipal Accountant exert more efforts to gather documents pertinent to the so-called reconciling figures to be able to identify them and make the necessary adjustment.  If no documents are found, adopt the bank balance as the correct balance if it is more than the book balance otherwise, the persons in-charge of preparing the bank reconciliation statements and recording cash transactions in the general ledger at the time the undetected reconciling items occurred will be held liable therefor.

2.5	We recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to persons not connected or permanent appointed official of the Municipality to avoid risk of non-liquidation of cash advances and possible loss of government funds thru misuse or misappropriation.

3.9	We recommended that the Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable. Avail of the legal remedies to collect from the delinquent real property taxpayers.

4.4	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Chairman and Members of the BAC to follow strictly the provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 or the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completely documented transactions/disbursements shall be paid.

5.4	We reiterated our recommendation that the:
Municipal Accountant maintain PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;
Designated Property Officer maintain property cards for all PPE; and
Inventory Committee exert extra effort to conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.

6.11	We recommended that the (a) BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices of the LGU of Moncada to (i) strictly monitor the  implementation of projects as targeted and planned and take note of any gap for future inputs; (ii) adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR; (b) Local Chief Executive (i) direct the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completely documented transactions/disbursements shall be paid; (ii) create a good internal communication protocols so that the concerned implementing office will become aware of the downloaded funds to avoid delays in the mobilization and implementation of projects ; (c) Municipal Agriculturist hasten (i) the submission of the additional documents required by DA for the release of fund for the Livestock Program ; (ii) the preparation of the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and farmers so that they can enjoy the benefits of the program at the earliest opportunity; (d) concerned Municipal Officials and Personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and examination.

7.4	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of drugs and medicines in the books using the Inventory account; (b) Concerned Offices to furnish copies of POs to the Audit Team within five days after issuance and copies of Inspection and Acceptance or its equivalent within 24 hours after their receipt of goods; and (c) Bids and Awards Committee to consolidate all procurement requests (PPMPs) and prepare the APP.

8.9	We recommended that the required Monthly Report of Utilization be prepared and submitted on a regular basis. We also recommended the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of P3,475,664.43 DRRM Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

9.5	We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) prioritize the implementation of projects, activities and programs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.

10.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the members of the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the annual School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and activities as stipulated in Section 1 of R.A. No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160, Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, and No. 01-A, March 14, 2000;  (b) stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of expenses not related to or included in the list of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF; and (c) strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No.  9184 and its RIRR.

11.7	We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate the Municipality’s own implementing guidelines, policies and procedures regarding AICS, in conformity with the provisions set forth by the DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014, as basis in the granting of financial or in-kind assistances to its constituents.

12.4	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in giving financial assistance (a) strictly adhere to the provisions set forth under DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014; and (b) use the assessment report of the MSWDO in determining the amount to be given to beneficiaries.

13.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated under Section 4.2 of DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014 to establish the validity of the claims; (b) make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the AICS; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the proper account is used in the payment of AICS.

14.7	We recommended that members of the Sangguniang Bayan: (a) initiate the conduct of a thorough study of the roads existing within the Municipality and determine the availability of alternative routes; and (b) enact an appropriate ordinance providing an exception to the existing prohibition of three-wheeled public conveyances operating along the national highways within the Municipality.

15.11	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all municipal officials involved in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and policies to improve the proficiencies of the GFPS and TWG by creating GAD Database and GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

16.6	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
17.2	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remit all withheld taxes intact and on time.

We reiterated our recommendation that the OIC, Municipal Accountant exert efforts to gather documents pertinent to the so-called reconciling figures to be able to identify them and make the necessary adjustment.  If no documents are found, adopt the bank balance as the correct balance if it is more than the book balance otherwise, the persons in-charge of preparing the bank reconciliation statements and recording cash transactions in the general ledger at the time the undetected reconciling items occurred will be held liable therefor.
observations.reason: Trust Fund’s balance per book was already reconciled with the balance per bank  as of December 31, 2016. However, the General Fund’s balance per book was not yet reconciled with the balance per bank, hence, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that - (a) the Municipal Mayor designate a regular employee or hire qualified personnel to perform the work of an accountable officer for the operation of OTOP-Don Benito Wine Production; (b) all daily cash collections be remitted daily and intact to the Municipal Treasurer; (c) payment of expenses incurred in wine production be made thru check or a regular cash advance for petty expenses may be drawn in favor of a bonded personnel; and (d) all sales/collections be properly acknowledged with an official receipt (AF # 51) to prevent loss of government funds thru illegal or other unauthorized use.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Petty Cash Custodian strictly follow the system in maintaining petty cash and in recording pertinent transactions as provided by COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 48, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant comply with COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 prescribing the guidelines on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of the confidential and/or intelligence funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representations with the bank and the legislator concerned for the release of the funds equivalent to the share of the Municipality from the collection of tobacco excise tax on the ground that such funds are no longer covered by the PDAF.

We commended the Municipality’s personnel for their efforts in actively participating in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003. We recommended, however, that they continue to sustain what have been put in place to carry out the law and to think of other ways to further improve their programs, projects and activities for better protection of the environment and the general welfare of the public.
We recommended that management continue the payment of loan amortization and regularly reconcile the balances of loans with the Land Bank of the Philippines.
